title: SilhouetteSplitFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing Silhouette Split features in a component and supports the ability to create new Silhouette Split features.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new silhouette split feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a SilhouetteSplitFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the silhouette split you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the SilhouetteSplitFeatureInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified silhouette split feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified silhouette split feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of Silhouette Split features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Silhouette Split Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new silhouette split feature.

add.title: SilhouetteSplitFeatures.add Method
add.description: Creates a new silhouette split feature.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created SilhouetteSplitFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A SilhouetteSplitFeatureInput object that defines the desired silhouette split feature. Use the createInput method to create a new SilhouetteSplitFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the SilhouetteSplitFeatureInput object) to define the silhouette split.

related_samples.name: silhouetteSplitFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the silhouetteSplitFeatures.add method. The Silhouette Split feature is limited in the bodies it will split. The simplest body to get a valid result is to create a sphere and split it.

related_samples.name: Silhouette Split Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new silhouette split feature.

classType.title: SilhouetteSplitFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: SilhouetteSplitFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a SilhouetteSplitFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the silhouette split you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the SilhouetteSplitFeatureInput object.
createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created SilhouetteSplitFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: viewDirection
parameters.description: A construction axis, linear BRepEdge, planar BRepFace or a construction plane that defines the view direction where the silhouette is calculated.

parameters.name: targetBody
parameters.description: Input the single solid body to split

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: The type of silhouette split operation to perform.

related_samples.name: silhouetteSplitFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the silhouetteSplitFeatures.add method. The Silhouette Split feature is limited in the bodies it will split. The simplest body to get a valid result is to create a sphere and split it.

related_samples.name: Silhouette Split Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new silhouette split feature.

item.title: SilhouetteSplitFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified silhouette split feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: SilhouetteSplitFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified silhouette split feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: SilhouetteSplitFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of Silhouette Split features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = silhouetteSplitFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: SilhouetteSplitFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = silhouetteSplitFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: SilhouetteSplitFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = silhouetteSplitFeatures_var.objectType
